item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: Control of the House of Representatives could come down to four pivotal battleground races in Pennsylvania

media:content.media:text: President Trump Touts Inflation Focus In Pennsylvania
media:content.media:description: Rep. Rob Bresnahan, a Republican from Pennsylvania, is in a battleground race against Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti this fall.

media:content.media:text: House GOP 3/4
media:content.media:description: Rep. Rob Bresnahan, a Republican from Pennsylvania, is in a battleground race against Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti this fall.

media:content.media:text: Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti in 2024.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Rob Bresnahan, a Republican from Pennsylvania, is in a battleground race against Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti this fall.

media:content.media:text: Brian Fitzpatrick 3/27/26
media:content.media:description: Rep. Rob Bresnahan, a Republican from Pennsylvania, is in a battleground race against Scranton Mayor Paige Cognetti this fall.

item.title: Four swing House races in Pennsylvania loom large for both parties — and for 2028, too

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Pilar Melendez
item.description: The two teenage suspects in this week’s deadly attack on a San Diego mosque appear to have written a 75-page document replete with neo-Nazi ideology, incel rage and racist meme culture drawn from the darkest corners of the internet

media:content.media:text: Islamic center of San Diego active shooter
media:content.media:description: Multiple people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered Islamic Center in San Diego Monday, May 18, 2026, and opened fire.

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Islamic Center Shooting
media:content.media:description: A body is covered with a tarp at the scene of a shooting outside the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

media:content.media:text: US-RELIGION-CRIME
media:content.media:description: Two women cry as they leave a reunification center following the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

media:content.media:text: People gather for a vigil outside the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday, the day after the shooting.
media:content.media:description: People gather for a vigil outside the Islamic Center of San Diego on Tuesday, the day after the shooting.

item.title: San Diego mosque suspects’ writings reveal influence of online extremism, experts say

item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche discusses the indictment of former Cuban President Raúl Castro, charged with the 1996 downing of two civilian planes. He called the move a "big step forward" for Cuban Americans.

media:content.media:title: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche says Castro indictment is 'big step forward'
media:content.media:description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche discusses the indictment of former Cuban President Raúl Castro, charged with the 1996 downing of two civilian planes. He called the move a "big step forward" for Cuban Americans.

item.title: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche says Castro indictment is 'big step forward'

item.description: SpaceX confirmed plans to sell shares of its stock to the public for the first time. NBC News' David Ingram has more on the developments.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX plans for a record-breaking IPO
media:content.media:description: SpaceX confirmed plans to sell shares of its stock to the public for the first time. NBC News' David Ingram has more on the developments.

item.title: SpaceX plans for a record-breaking IPO

item.description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa
media:content.media:description: The American doctor who tested positive for Ebola while working in Congo has been evacuated to Berlin to receive emergency care. Health officials are racing to trace infections in Central Africa and worry the scale of the outbreak is already larger than first reported. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

item.title: Health Officials Race to Trace Rising Ebola Cases in Central Africa

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: Durbin, the top Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, asked acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to provide more details about Trump’s $1.8B “anti-weaponization” fund.

media:content.media:text: Noem Senate Judiciary Hearing
media:content.media:description: Sen. Dick Durbin, D-Ill., at the Dirksen Senate Office Building in Washington in March.

media:content.media:text: Todd Blanche testifies while seated
media:content.media:description: Sen. Dick Durbin, D-Ill., at the Dirksen Senate Office Building in Washington in March.

item.title: Sen. Dick Durbin warns DOJ against ‘doling out compensation to rioters’ in new Trump fund

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Joe Kottke
Alex Lo
item.description: An Air France flight bound for Detroit was redirected to Canada after a passenger from Congo boarded “in error” amid the Ebola outbreak in central Africa, officials said.

media:content.media:text: An Air France plane
media:content.media:description: An Air France plane in May.

item.title: Detroit-bound flight diverted to Canada after passenger from Congo boarded ‘in error’ amid Ebola outbreak

item.dc:creator: Casey Wooten
item.description: Colorado Democrats voted to censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting the prison sentence of Tina Peters, an election denier who had been serving nine years in state prison for tampering with voting systems.

media:content.media:text: Colorado Gov. Jared Polis
media:content.media:description: Along with the censure, Colorado Gov. Jared Polis won't be able to participate in state party events.

item.title: Colorado Democrats censure Gov. Jared Polis for commuting election denier’s sentence

item.dc:creator: Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: A former federal prosecutor was indicted on charges she emailed herself confidential files from special counsel Jack Smith’s investigation of allegations that President Donald Trump mishandled classified documents, the Justice Department said.

media:content.media:text: Jack Smith House Hearing
media:content.media:description: Former Special Counsel Jack Smith testifies during a House Judiciary Committee oversight hearing, in Washington, D.C., in Jan.

item.title: Former prosecutor emailed herself the unreleased Jack Smith report, DOJ alleges

item.dc:creator: Morgan Chesky
Tim Stelloh
item.description: Like schools across the U.S., the Islamic Center of San Diego had sought to prepare its students for the possibility that a gunman could breach its walls.

media:content.media:text: Islamic center of San Diego active shooter
media:content.media:description: Three people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday and opened fire.

media:content.media:text: At Least 3 Killed In Shooting At Islamic Center of San Diego
media:content.media:description: Three people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday and opened fire.

media:content.media:text: Community holds vigil after deadly mosque shooting in San Diego
media:content.media:description: Three people were killed, including an armed security guard, after two shooters entered the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday and opened fire.

item.title: Teacher’s assistant at San Diego mosque recalls students’ bravery as gunmen banged on doors

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Vanessa Trump, the ex-wife of Donald Trump Jr., has been diagnosed with breast cancer, she said.

media:content.media:text: Vanessa Trump
media:content.media:description: Vanessa Trump and her daughter Kai Trump in Palm Beach Gardens, Fla., in March.

item.title: Vanessa Trump reveals breast cancer diagnosis

item.dc:creator: Emily Lorsch
item.description: As Meta cut 10% of its workforce on Wednesday, CEO Mark Zuckerberg addressed the tech giant’s transition in a companywide memo.

media:content.media:text: Zuckerberg To Defend Meta's Youth Strategy At Addiction Trial
media:content.media:description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg leaves Los Angeles County Superior Court in February.

item.title: Zuckerberg warns ‘success isn’t a given’ after laying off 10% of Meta

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: Durbin, the top Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, asked acting Attorney General Todd Blanche to provide more details about Trump’s $1.8B “anti-weaponization” fund.

media:content.media:text: Noem Senate Judiciary Hearing

item.title: Sen. Dick Durbin warns DOJ against ‘doling out compensation to rioters’ in new Trump fund

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Andrea Mitchell examines the next chapter in U.S.-Cuba relations after Raúl Castro’s indictment.

media:content.media:text: Raul Castro
media:content.media:description: Cuba's First Secretary of Communist Party Raul Castro gives a speech during the celebration of 60th Anniversary of Cuban Revolution, in Santiago de Cuba, Cuba.

item.title: Raúl Castro’s indictment marks important new chapter for the U.S. and Cuba: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency said residents should feel assured that most properties cleared by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers don’t have hazardous amounts of lead. At least one outside scientist is skeptical.

media:content.media:text: altadena fire cleanup

item.title: Los Angeles-area wildfires left lead in soil, but how much and where remains contentious

item.dc:creator: Carmen Sesin
item.description: Cuba shot down two Brothers to the Rescue U.S. civilian planes in 1996, killing four Cuban Americans and drawing swift condemnation from the U.S. and the international community.

media:content.media:text: Cuba Brothers to the Rescue.
media:content.media:description: Boats from a flotilla head for open waters off Key West, Fla., on March 2, 1996, carrying Cuban Americans to the site of the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes by Cuban fighters.

media:content.media:text: Mirta Costa Méndez stands next to the image of her late brother, Carlos Costa, one of the men killed in the shoot down of the Brothers to the Rescue planes in 1996. Melisa Chantres, on the right, is his niece.
media:content.media:description: Boats from a flotilla head for open waters off Key West, Fla., on March 2, 1996, carrying Cuban Americans to the site of the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes by Cuban fighters.

media:content.media:text: Photos of Brothers to the Rescue pilots Carlos Costa, Amando Alejandre Jr., Mario de la Peña and Pablo Morales.
media:content.media:description: Boats from a flotilla head for open waters off Key West, Fla., on March 2, 1996, carrying Cuban Americans to the site of the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue planes by Cuban fighters.

item.title: Raúl Castro indictment brings back history of Cuba’s downing of U.S. civilian planes

item.dc:creator: Allie Canal
item.description: The company behind snack brands including Lay’s and Doritos said it’s raising prices on some of its smaller chip bags.

media:content.media:text: A bag of Lay's Classic potato chips.

item.title: Lay’s and Doritos maker to raise prices on small bags of chips

item.dc:creator: Bianca Alvarez
item.description: Repair your skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended creams from brands including La Roche Posay, CeraVe, Drunk Elephant, Avène, Tatcha and more.

media:content.media:text: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.
media:content.media:description: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Bianca Alvarez, NBC Select Associate Reporter
media:content.media:text: NBC Select associate reporter Bianca Alvarez swatches the peach-tinted cream on her hand to show the thick and rich consistency of the moisturizer.
media:content.media:description: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Bianca Alvarez, NBC Select Associate Reporter
media:content.media:text: Alvarez squeezes out the Tower 28 SOS Daily Barrier Recovery Cream. In the picture on the right, Alvarez applies the moisturizer to her face.
media:content.media:description: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Zoe Malin, NBC Select Associate Updates Editor
media:content.media:text: NBC Select associate updates editor Zoe Malin holds and applies the thin and white La Roche Posay Toleriane Double Repair Face Moisturizer to her face.
media:content.media:description: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.

media:content.media:text: Skinfix's Barrier Cream has one of the most nourishing formulas on our list, thanks to a combination of humectants, emollients and occlusives.
media:content.media:description: Repair and maintain a healthy skin barrier with dermatologist-recommended barrier creams from brands like Drunk Elephant, Kiehl’s, La Roche Posay and CeraVe.

item.title: Have you been damaging your skin barrier without knowing it?

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz

item.dc:creator: Deanna Pai
item.description: Retinal is more potent and faster-acting than retinol, and I asked experts for the best picks from Youth to the People, Beauty of Joseon, Naturium and more.

media:content.media:text: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.
media:content.media:description: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.

media:content.media:text: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.
media:content.media:description: Retinal can still cause irritation, so dermatologists recommend looking for fragrance-free versions with hydrating ingredients.

item.title: Which is better — retinal or retinol?

item.title: 68+ best sales we plan to shop this week: Ilia, J. Crew and more

item.description: The graduation for Glendale Community College in Arizona was disrupted when an AI name-reading system apparently began skipping some students’ names during the degree presentation.

media:content.media:title: Graduation ceremony disrupted by AI name-reading system
media:content.media:description: The graduation for Glendale Community College in Arizona was disrupted when an AI name-reading system apparently began skipping some students’ names during the degree presentation.

item.title: Graduation ceremony disrupted by AI name-reading system
